resources.string:
Nutrition Advice

1. Fruit Salad
A mix of fresh fruits like bananas, apples, and oranges.

2. Grilled Chicken
Low-fat grilled chicken served with vegetables.

3. Vegetable Stir Fry
Carrots, broccoli, and peppers cooked lightly.

4. Oatmeal Breakfast
Oats with milk and fruits.

5. Avocado Toast
Whole grain bread topped with avocado.

6. Boiled Eggs
Rich in protein and easy to prepare.

7. Smoothie Bowl
Blended fruits with yogurt and nuts.

8. Brown Rice and Beans
Healthy and full of fiber.

9. Baked Fish
Fish baked with lemon and spices.

10. Greek Yogurt with Honey
Good for digestion and very nutritious.

Meditation Content

1. Mindful Breathing
Focus on your breath, inhaling and exhaling slowly to calm your mind.

2. Body Scan Meditation
Gently bring awareness to each part of your body from head to toe.

3. Guided Visualization
Imagine a peaceful place like a beach or forest to relax your thoughts.

4. Loving-Kindness Meditation
Silently repeat phrases wishing well-being for yourself and others.

5. Mantra Meditation
Repeat a calming word or phrase to maintain focus and clarity.

6. Walking Meditation
Walk slowly and focus on each step and movement of your body.

7. Gratitude Meditation
Reflect on things you are thankful for to cultivate positivity.

8. Silent Sitting
Sit quietly and observe your thoughts without judgment.

9. Chakra Meditation
Focus on different energy centers in your body for balance.

10. Progressive Relaxation
Tense and relax each muscle group to release physical stress.

Start Exercise

1. Warm-Up Stretch
Gently stretch your muscles to prepare your body for activity.

2. Jumping Jacks
A simple full-body exercise to increase heart rate.

3. Bodyweight Squats
Strengthen your legs and improve balance.

4. Push-Ups
Build upper body strength using your own weight.

5. Plank Hold
Engage your core and improve stability.

6. Lunges
Work your legs and improve coordination.

7. High Knees
Boost cardio and activate lower body muscles.

8. Mountain Climbers
A great full-body workout for strength and endurance.

9. Cool Down Stretch
Slowly stretch to relax muscles after exercise.

10. Deep Breathing
Calm your body and restore normal breathing.

Check Progress
Fruit Mix Bowl – A refreshing combination of seasonal fruits that provides natural sugars and vitamins. Lean Grilled Chicken – Lightly seasoned chicken cooked without excess oil, perfect for muscle health. Sautéed Vegetables – A colorful mix of lightly cooked veggies that retain nutrients and flavor. Warm Oats Meal – A filling breakfast made with oats, offering long-lasting energy throughout the day. Avocado Bread Slice – Crunchy whole grain bread layered with creamy avocado for healthy fats. Hard-Boiled Eggs – Simple protein-rich eggs that help keep you full and energized. Creamy Fruit Blend – A thick mix of blended fruits, yogurt, and crunchy toppings for added texture. Whole Grain Rice and Legumes – A balanced dish packed with fiber and plant-based protein.☐ Oven-Cooked Fish – Gently baked fish infused with herbs for a heart-friendly meal. Yogurt with Natural Sweetness – Smooth yogurt paired with a touch of honey to support gut health.
